mnemonics: PIE Principle: Principle of Inclusion-Exclusion (Add groups, subtract overlaps, add back higher overlaps)
keyPoints: The Rule of Product: If there are m ways to do one thing and n ways to do another, there are m*n ways to do both
The Rule of Sum: If two tasks are mutually exclusive, the total ways is the sum of the ways for each task
The Pigeonhole Principle: If you have more items than containers, at least one container must hold more than one item
commonMistakes: Confusing when to use the product rule versus the sum rule (AND vs OR logic)
Forgetting to subtract the overlapping cases when using the inclusion-exclusion principle for non-mutually exclusive sets
Misapplying the pigeonhole principle by not correctly identifying the 'pigeons' and 'holes'
